validation.command: node --check src/control-plane/actions.js; node --check src/control-plane/runs.js; node --check src/runtime.js; node --check test/lifecycle-ownership.test.js
validation.summary: Syntax checks passed for the T023 action/runtime/test surfaces.
21 tests run, 17 passed, 4 failed. Remaining failures are CLI/output routing or JSON payload shape residuals owned by T024.

controllerChecks: Ownership-gated stop now refuses probable-owned, unknown, external, and matching-PID/listener-without-trusted-spawn-proof cases without killing the listener.
Verified stop for Launchdeck-spawned processes uses a trusted spawn proof or current verified ownership evidence; PID/port/local-state-only evidence is not upgraded to verified ownership.
Force semantics were not allowed to grant ownership and no CLI force-stop route was added in this task.
Stop and restart action-layer paths acquire project/task locks through the existing lock surfaces.
Stopped and stop_failed state/event evidence is persisted through runtime evidence.
Source-code sniffing for stop failure was removed; stop_failed is based on process liveness, declared port listeners, stop result, and timeout evidence.
Leader process scan found no lifecycle test fixture processes left after validation.
acceptedResidualRisks: T024 must route stop/force-stop/restart through the action-layer commands and normalize JSON payloads/error codes.

The remaining failing lifecycle tests are expected until T024 updates CLI/output wiring: stop payload stopped[]/failed[], force-stop routing, restart stoppedRun/startedRun/transactionId, and port_release_timeout mapping.
Cross-platform stop semantics remain best-effort and must still be covered by later smoke evidence before a release claim.
followUpWork: T024 wire lifecycle commands and refusal next actions in src/cli.js, src/output.js, and src/errors.js.
T025 join US3 owned stop/restart validation after T024.
